META.reasoning: CATALYST WINDOW: Q1 2026 earnings fired 1 trading day ago (April 29 AH) — we are inside the 1-3 day post-catalyst re-rating window. This is the dominant catalyst. NET NEWS SENTIMENT: NEGATIVE. EPS beat 57% and revenue beat +33% YoY were overshadowed by a massive capex guidance raise to $125-145B (nearly double FY2025's $72B), confirming the bear case thesis of free cash flow compression. DAP growth slowed to +4% YoY / down >5% QoQ. 8,000 layoffs (10% of workforce) announced — signals cost pressure is real. Social media addiction litigation (California 70% liability verdict, 10,000+ lawsuits, $375M New Mexico award) remains a persistent high-negative overhang. Muse Spark AI narrative losing ground vs Alphabet's stronger AI monetization story. Some analysts moved to Hold post-Q1 with $700-range targets. INSIDER ACTIVITY: Quiet — no insider trades in the last 30 days. No insider buying to suggest confidence ahead of earnings. TECHNICAL SETUP (poor/broken — not constructive): Price gapped down from $669.12 to $611.91 on 3.34x average volume — heavy institutional distribution. Price is below ALL moving averages on both daily and hourly timeframes. Daily ADX 49.21 with -DI (34.51) dominating +DI (26.27): strong confirmed downtrend. Hourly ADX 30.67, -DI 49.52 — even stronger bearish trend on shorter timeframe. OBV trending down, volume bias 10d: bearish. Hourly Bollinger pct_b at -0.0083 (at/below lower band). Daily RSI-7 at 23.55 and hourly RSI-21 at 24.16 indicate oversold conditions — a dead-cat bounce is possible — but daily RSI divergence (bullish: true) has NOT been confirmed with a price follow-through. MACD remains in deep negative territory on both timeframes. ROC-5d: -7.17%, ROC-10d: -9.6% — momentum firmly bearish. LESSONS CHECK: Recent EMA pullback Fib dip-buy on NVDA failed (-$264.65, stop triggered at hourly Fib 61.8%); a similar dip-buy thesis here would be analogous and risky given the stronger negative catalyst. STRATEGY: Short entry on a relief bounce toward the earnings-gap breakdown zone ($630-635), targeting Fib 61.8% at $585.68 (daily). Stop above the gap-fill zone at $645 to limit risk if institutions step in for a full gap fill. No binary catalyst within the next 10 trading days (Q2 2026 earnings expected late July) — this is a post-earnings multiple compression / institutional re-rating drift play. Confidence reduced from 85 to 78 due to extreme oversold RSI (bounce risk) and large bid at $600 hourly pivot (volume-confirmed support).
